extraction.one_line_summary: BISSIGER Stefan dominated the 9km Ajman ITT by 7 seconds over GANNA, with POGAČAR winning 11 seconds on YATES to control GC before the mountain stage.
tactical_patterns: BISSIGER's 9:43 at 9km suggests specialized short-ITT riders outperform pure power TTers when technical corners dominate course profile.
POGAČAR gained 11 seconds on YATES in under 10 minutes, revealing meaningful GC separation possible even in ultra-short prologue formats.
DUMOULIN's return from layoff yielded third at +14s, indicating form building but still 7s off winner in specialist distance.

rider_signals.BISSIGER Stefan: positive: Held hot seat for over an hour against world champion, suggesting EF tactics should prioritize him in future short ITTs over climbers
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: neutral: World champion beaten by 7s on 9km technical course indicates corners/turns erode his pure wattage advantage versus lighter specialists
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Sub-10-minute ride with 11s margin on YATES preempts mountain stage, showing ITT discipline even in trivial distances
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: Second at intermediate split and sub-10:05 finish reveals unexpected short-TT strength for climber category
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: positive: Third place post-layoff confirms rebuilding trajectory faster than expected for multi-month absence comeback
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: Lost 11s to POGAČAR in 9km suggests vulnerable in any race with even token ITT mileage against complete riders
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: neutral: Sprinter holding leader's jersey until ITT indicates early bunch finishes, relinquishing to BISSIGER confirms expected GC reset
